items.reason: DEC-001 through DEC-020 all populate id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to. The new platform-shape decisions (DEC-016 dictionary direction, DEC-017 cross-spec reconciliation, DEC-018 global enums, DEC-019 closed privacy classes, DEC-020 migration direction) are present and each artifact they imply (data/data-dictionary.seed.json, scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py, shared_enums tables, privacy_classes list, scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py) is in the codebase, with non-empty projects_to lists pointing to concrete dictionary entries and migration paths.

docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md lists PEND-001 (target 2026-05-15, owner Andy, blocker product priority) and PEND-005 (target 2026-05-03, owner Andy, blocker CI capacity). Both have owner, target_date, blocker, and one-line rationale; both target dates are in the future relative to the research date 2026-04-24/26.

Each consequences section names a concrete artifact, code path, or category that the decision removes. E.g. DEC-001 'Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts'; DEC-010 'Removes duplicated tenant where tenant_id = ... guard code from PostgREST callers'; DEC-016 'Removes direct edits to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json ... as an accepted workflow'. Decisions also include 'Why not collapse' justifications when preserving multiple surfaces.

Each decision lists three credible alternatives bracketing the design space. DEC-001 considers separate per-spec identity tables (a real LMS pattern), full collapse into person (a real single-tenant pattern), and the chosen agent+person split. DEC-005 considers AGS-as-canonical, peer source-native stores, and result+scores+events separation. DEC-016/017/018 each contrast shared-upstream, per-spec-upstream, and the chosen direction — all are architectures shipped by other production platforms.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json carries projection_summary {total_objects: 59, total_fields: 575} and contains canonical_objects plus per-spec object/field definitions. scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py produces dictionary/*.v1.json and exposes --check, which is run in VERIFY.md. check_dictionary_artifacts.py also invokes the generator with --check as a drift gate. The seed is not a stub.

canonical_objects in the seed declare canonical.identity.person.email with projections list pointing to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json#person.email, dictionary/caliper-core.v1.json#caliper_actor.email, dictionary/qti-core.v1.json#qti_candidate.email, and dictionary/integration-governance-core.v1.json#lti_membership.email — all four overlap fields share one canonical_field_id. Same pattern applies to display_name, source_subject_id, and other shared concepts. The validator in generate_spec_dictionaries.py also accepts many-to-one canonical_field_id binding for cross-spec joins.

canonical_objects[0] in the seed defines canonical.identity.person with its own fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind), allowed_values for identity_kind, and a projections array binding OneRoster Person, Caliper actor, QTI Candidate, LTI membership, and LTI launch.subject_id. The unification is data, not prose. spec_only_projections explicitly documents the QTI candidate exception against DEC-012/PEND-001.

Each per-spec dictionary carries a top-level shared_enums array with canonical_values, crosswalk (spec-native -> canonical), and reverse_crosswalk (canonical -> all spec-native values). For example, grading_progress crosswalks OneRoster result.score_status fullyGraded/partiallyGraded/etc. and LTI lti_grade_exchange.grading_progress FullyGraded/Pending/PendingManual/Failed/NotReady to one canonical set, both directions. check_dictionary_artifacts.py validates that every enum field carries shared_enum_id and appears in the crosswalk.

DEC-019 names the closed list (public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system) and bans depends_on_entity / depends_on_contents. scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py validate_privacy_classes() rejects values starting with depends_on_ as INVALID_PRIVACY_CLASSES, walks every object/field/spec_field/canonical-object-field, and fails projection if any unknown class appears. This makes the closure executable, not merely declared.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json migration_generation block declares table_order, runtime_object_keys, and relationship_keys (e.g., class_course, enrollment_class, line_item_class, result_line_item, source_identifier_object). scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py reads relationships[] arrays on each runtime object and emits supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql, with --check (run in VERIFY.md and in check_dictionary_artifacts.py) failing on drift. validate_graph enforces that every FK relationship cites DEC-020 and matches required-field flags.

scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py REQUIRED_FIELD_KEYS includes decision_id; it also loads docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md decision-trace JSON block and fails if any field references an unknown decision_id or is missing from that decision's produces_fields list. Allowed values likewise carry decision_id (e.g. shared_enums canonical_values cite DEC-018). Orphans are blocked at CI.

scripts/generate_spec_fidelity_report.py exists and writes docs/spec-fidelity-report.md; VERIFY.md runs both `--report-only` and `--check`. The script enforces, on the 1EdTech side, json_name == sourceStandard.field and column_name == snake_case(sourceStandard.field), exits non-zero on silent renames, and treats missing extension_rationale on extension fields as a hard --check failure (collect_missing_rationales). VERIFY currently passes per the loop, so the seam is provable from generated artifacts and 0 silent renames remain.

scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py, generate_qti_core.py, generate_case_core.py, generate_caliper_core.py, generate_integration_governance_core.py emit Markdown, OpenAPI, SQL comments, and HTML directly from dictionary/*.v1.json. scripts/build_site_docs.py renders the rest. check_dictionary_artifacts.py asserts that every object/field/value description in dictionaries appears in the generated SQL/OpenAPI/Markdown/HTML, blocking stale prose. Hand-edited prose pages (build-an-edtech-app.md, lead-spec-accounting.md) link to dictionary anchors per field reference.

docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md renders to site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html with per-decision anchors (verified by site-link-check.json: 20 decisions checked). docs/lead-spec-accounting.md carries a 'Decision Citation Map' table tying each Lead spec accounting entry to its decision_id and the simplification it produced. docs/dictionary-coverage-matrix.md does the same. Each decision's prose explains the choice and what it deletes in plain language.

docs/build-an-edtech-app.md walks all five required steps (school+teacher+10 students, class+enrollment, numeric grade, CASE URI alignment via line_items.case_target_uri, Caliper feed via class_activity_feed) with copy-pasteable curl against a real Supabase project, including a self-hosted-sandbox path with the migration command for reviewers without dashboard access. Every field and enum value referenced has a dictionary link table prepended to the step.

site/api/site-link-check.json reports status: pass, errors: 0, html_pages: 37, reachable_html_pages: 37, max_clicks: 3, dictionary_entries_checked: 634, decisions_checked: 20, api_endpoints_checked: 12. The buildability guide is reachable in 1 click from the portal. Every dictionary anchor expected by the checker resolves.

Reading only docs/, a layperson can produce executable curl for all five test steps. Step 0 names the exact dashboard path for SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY and provides a self-hosted alternative loading supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql. Steps 1–5 each include complete request bodies, dictionary anchors for every field/enum value used, and a verification curl. tenant_id is documented as a JWT-claim default per DEC-010 so request bodies don't need it. CASE alignment uses the line_items.case_target_uri runtime field; Caliper feed reads the class_activity_feed view. No spec-conversion knowledge is required.

This evaluator output sets traced_cause to the empty string for every pass item per the schema rule, and would name the lowest-layer artifact for any non-pass item. There are no non-pass items in this run, so backward tracing is vacuously satisfied; the harness contract is still enforced because the JSON shape requires non-empty traced_cause whenever status != pass.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, so each iteration writes a fresh report. scripts/codex_loop.py also calls run_evaluator() before and after each iteration and parses the JSON to check counts and `done`. The published JSON is required by the harness to have per-item status and traced_cause.

scripts/codex_loop.py reads docs/loop-overrides.md via read_overrides() at the top of every iteration, then calls overrides_paused() BEFORE running Codex; if PAUSE is on its own line (with HTML comments stripped) the loop returns 0 immediately, halting before the next iteration runs. build_prompt() injects the cleaned overrides under a 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE — these supersede rubric defaults...)' header.

After VERIFY.md runs (which writes the authoritative platform-evaluation.json), codex_loop.py calls run_evaluator(label='after') to re-read site/api/platform-evaluation.json and only stops when after_eval.get('done') is true. The driver explicitly handles 'LOOP_STATUS: COMPLETE' in Codex's transcript by checking the verifier first: if Codex claims complete but evaluator disagrees, it logs 'Codex reported COMPLETE but evaluator says work remains. Continuing.' and proceeds to the next iteration. Stall detection uses rubric_progressed() on counts, not score thresholds.

maybe_publish() in scripts/codex_loop.py runs `git fetch origin main` then `git pull --rebase origin main` before `git push origin main`. On rebase failure it captures conflicting paths from `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U`, runs `git rebase --abort`, and returns a string starting with 'DIVERGENCE:' that names the paths and the recommended `git fetch && git pull --rebase && git push` resolution. The main loop checks `if publish_result.startswith('DIVERGENCE:'): return 1` immediately, so the harness halts at N=1 on any rejected push and writes the divergence to PROGRESS.md via append_machine_progress.

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. The teaching-app runtime also backs the narrow CASE alignment path needed by gradebook line items (`line_items.case_target_uri`) and the narrow Caliper class-feed path needed by the buildability guide (`caliper_events` plus `class_activity_feed`). QTI and full CASE import/search remain doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated table, view, Edge Function receipt, or governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, full Sensor API ingestion/projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.
